L. 103–236, title V, § 506(c)108 Stat. 464Apr. 30, 1994Nov. 20, 1994“The amendments made by this section [enacting this chapter] shall take effect on the later of—“(1)the date of enactment of this Act []; orApr. 30, 1994“(2)the date on which the United States has become a party to the Convention Against Torture and Other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment.”

children.text: “torture” means an act committed by a person acting under the color of law specifically intended to inflict severe physical or mental pain or suffering (other than pain or suffering incidental to lawful sanctions) upon another person within his custody or physical control;

children.label: A
children.text: the intentional infliction or threatened infliction of severe physical pain or suffering;

children.label: B
children.text: the administration or application, or threatened administration or application, of mind-altering substances or other procedures calculated to disrupt profoundly the senses or the personality;

children.label: C
children.text: the threat of imminent death; or

children.label: D
children.text: the threat that another person will imminently be subjected to death, severe physical pain or suffering, or the administration or application of mind-altering substances or other procedures calculated to disrupt profoundly the senses or personality; and
“United States” means the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, and the commonwealths, territories, and possessions of the United States.
